description: Config for scripts/check-doc-drift.mjs — codifies Class #9 (multi-occurrence sibling-fix sweep) and Class #11 (multi-occurrence fix completeness). Each entry is one drift CHECK. Adding a new check is a config edit, not a code edit: append a new object with { id, kind, title, ... } where the remaining fields are the requiredFields declared by the handler module for that kind. The framework registers seven handler modules — source-vs-target-coverage, forbidden-pattern-in-targets, self-consistency, untagged-fence, helper-adoption-sweep, schema-conformance, framework-self-test — and dispatches each entry to the matching handler. New drift CLASSES (entirely new kinds of comparison) need both a config entry AND a new handler module; new INSTANCES of an existing class are config-only.

checks.title: All error codes documented
checks.description: Every error.code value emitted from packages/**/lib/errors.js (including STATE_MACHINE_<KIND> expansions) must appear in the error-codes handbook page.
checks.hint: Document the missing error code in site/src/content/docs/handbook/error-codes.md following the existing format (### `CODE_NAME`, Class, Trigger, Message shape, Hint, Operator action).

checks.title: All STATUS.* enum values documented
checks.description: Every status string in the STATUS object exported from packages/dogfood-swarm/db/schema.js must be mentioned somewhere in the state-machines handbook page. The wave classifier states (new/recurring/fixed/unverified/...) and finding-review states (candidate/reviewed/accepted/...) are the public vocabularies operators need named.
checks.hint: Mention the missing status in site/src/content/docs/handbook/state-machines.md. The state-machines page documents PUBLIC state vocabularies (record classification, finding review, wave classification). Internal control-plane plumbing (run/wave/agent_run statuses) is exempt via skipKeys in scripts/doc-drift-patterns.json. If a finding-review or wave-classifier state is genuinely internal-only and shouldn't be documented, add it to the allowlist with a comment explaining why.

checks.title: No legacy paths in user-facing docs
checks.description: User-facing docs (handbook + docs/) must not contain legacy paths. The README and CLAUDE.md retain explicit historical references on purpose (per CLAUDE.md 'Working with the legacy' section).
checks.hint: Replace the legacy reference with the canonical equivalent (dogfood-lab/testing-os, packages/<name>/, repo-relative path). Historical context belongs in README.md / CLAUDE.md / HANDOFF.md, NOT in user-facing handbook pages.

checks.title: No version-specific terms that should be parameterized
checks.description: Narrative docs should not hardcode counts that move between releases (e.g. '9-Phase' when the protocol is now 10 phases). Code snippets, CHANGELOG, and the version-stamp block in README are exempt.

patterns.label: stale 9-Phase reference (protocol is 10-phase since Stage D added)

checks.hint: Update the count to the current value. If you need to refer to a historical state, scope the reference (e.g. 'pre-Stage-D, the protocol was 9 phases') rather than asserting the current count is wrong.

checks.title: Every opening code fence has a language tag
checks.description: Stage D wave 23 (D-CI-001 / F-827321-010): every ```fence in site/src/content/docs/handbook/*.md must declare a language so Starlight's Shiki applies syntax highlighting, the copy-button, and consistent border treatment. Bare ``` openers render as plain monospace and read as draft-quality next to tagged blocks. Use ```text for ASCII / CLI output / state diagrams, ```bash / ```yaml / ```json / ```ts for code. The handler walks each target line-by-line, toggling an in-fence flag, and only flags openers — closing fences are correctly bare.
checks.hint: Add a language tag to the opening fence: ```text for ASCII diagrams or CLI output, ```bash for shell commands, ```yaml / ```json / ```ts / ```js for code, ```mermaid if/when the Mermaid integration lands. Closing fences stay bare.

checks.title: PROTOCOL.md stage references are internally consistent
checks.description: PROTOCOL.md mentions Stages A–D in the Health Pass plus Phase 5–8 (Feature), Phase 9 (Final), Phase 10 (Full Treatment). Every Stage and Phase referenced in narrative must also be defined as a section/header. Drift here means the title or summary moved without the body following.

must.label: Stage D must be defined with 'Visual' lens
Stage D must appear as a bolded entry in the stage list

checks.hint: Update PROTOCOL.md so the title block, the stage list, the per-phase audit lens enumeration, and the coordinator checklist all refer to the same set of stages. Class #11 sweep pattern: fix every occurrence in one pass, never one-by-one.

checks.title: atomic-write helper adopted by all writers under packages/
checks.description: Every callsite that writes a file via raw fs.writeFileSync should go through atomicWriteFileSync from packages/findings/lib/atomic-write.js to avoid torn writes. Helper history: F-721047-010 sibling-fix gap. The allowlist captures known cross-fix-dependencies — Phase 7 wave 2 backend will retire each of these. Test fixtures (*.test.js) are auto-excluded.
checks.wrapperHint: Cross-package: import via @dogfood-lab/findings/lib/atomic-write.js. Same package: relative import. Wrappers that pin a component tag are allowed; the wrapper file must still import the helper. Phase 7 wave 2 W2-BACK-003: 6 dogfood-swarm/ callers migrated to import the helper; allowlist shrunk from 13 to 6 entries (5 cross-domain pipeline/outputs callers + the helper itself remain). Verify/runner.js was confirmed STALE (no callsites in source) and removed entirely.

checks.title: log-stage helper is the SOLE definition of logStage()
checks.description: Productizes wave22-log-stage-discipline.test.js as a config-driven sweep. The shared helper at packages/dogfood-swarm/lib/log-stage.js is the canonical logStage; sibling files importing it (with optional component-pinning wrappers) are clean. Forbidden pattern catches `function logStage(`, `const logStage =`, etc. anywhere except the helper.
checks.wrapperHint: Use `import { logStage } from '@dogfood-lab/dogfood-swarm/lib/log-stage.js'`. If a wrapper is needed (e.g. ingest's component-pinning), the wrapper file must still import the shared helper.

checks.title: RecordValidationError is the only validation-error class for persisted records
checks.description: validate-record.js owns the contract. F-252713-017 cross-ref: AgentOutputValidationError (used by schema-conformance handler) mirrors this shape. Callers that throw their own validation error class instead of importing RecordValidationError create a parallel error vocabulary that operators have to learn twice.
checks.wrapperHint: Import { RecordValidationError } from the validate-record helper rather than redefining the class.

checks.title: Path-segment safety check is the central helper
checks.description: Per F-916867-005, only one of three path-segment validation callsites had adopted the central helper. Until the helper is extracted to a shared module, this entry is allowlist-only and serves as a tracking anchor. When packages/dogfood-swarm/lib/path-safety.js (or equivalent) lands with the canonical unsafeSegment(), this entry's helper / exportName / forbiddenPattern fields update and the allowlist shrinks to just the helper. Today the entry uses atomic-write as the placeholder helper since unsafeSegment hasn't been extracted yet — the allowlist captures every file that defines its own unsafeSegment.
checks.wrapperHint: F-916867-005 follow-up: extract the canonical unsafeSegment() to a shared module (likely packages/dogfood-swarm/lib/path-safety.js) and update this config entry's helper/exportName fields. Until then, every additional callsite must be added to the allowlist with a cross-ref.

checks.title: Structured error envelope (typed errors with .code) is the single shape
checks.description: Errors that cross module boundaries should carry .code so callers can pattern-match without substring grep on .message. The canonical examples are IsolationError / CollectUpsertError / StateMachineRejectionError in packages/dogfood-swarm/lib/errors.js. New typed errors should live there or import from there. This sweep flags any sibling re-defining IsolationError outside the helper.
checks.wrapperHint: Import { IsolationError } from the central errors module. New typed errors with .code go into packages/dogfood-swarm/lib/errors.js (or a sibling errors.js if they belong to a different package's lifecycle).

checks.title: Agent JSON outputs (audit / feature / amend) conform to scripts/agent-output.schema.json
checks.description: The canonical envelope is { domain, summary, [findings|features|fixes], ... }. Phase-7-wave-1 productization (F-252713-017): replaces silent normalization in collect.js with a structured AgentOutputValidationError pointing the agent at the canonical shape. The schema is permissive at top level (only `domain` + `summary` required) so it can govern audit, feature, AND amend outputs, with phase-specific $defs for the inner shapes. Targets a fixture path today — the live wiring is a cross-wave dependency on backend (Phase 7 wave 2 will (a) extend the categories enum to absorb historical wave-15/wave-20 categories or migrate those files, and (b) wire the validator into collect.js's mergeFindings flow).
checks.hint: Match scripts/agent-output.schema.json. Required at top level: domain, summary. Audit outputs add `findings[]` (with severity ∈ CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW and category ∈ AUDIT_CATEGORIES); feature outputs add `features[]`; amend outputs add `fixes[]` + `files_changed[]`. Once backend wave 2 wires this into collect.js, the target glob expands to `swarms/swarm-*/wave-*/*.json` (after the historical-shape migration lands).

checks.title: check-doc-drift framework structure is internally consistent
checks.description: Meta-check: every config entry has a registered handler and all required fields. Drift here means someone added a check kind in config but forgot to register the handler module, or removed a required field from an existing check. The choke-point invariant — the framework checks itself.
checks.hint: Either register the missing handler module in scripts/check-doc-drift.mjs (HANDLERS map), or remove the orphaned check from scripts/doc-drift-patterns.json. Required fields are declared on each handler module.
